items.question: Has the centre clearly identified its service category such as day care, pre-primary, nursery/kindergarten, community-based, home-based or attached centre?
items.action: Confirm the service category because registration, licensing, inspection and standards requirements depend on the type of ECCE provision.
items.source_label: ECCE Policy and Standards on ECCE categories and service delivery

items.question: Are ownership, management and responsible persons clearly documented?
items.action: Prepare ownership and management records, including the person responsible for day-to-day centre operations.
items.source_label: ECCE Policy provisions on establishment and management of ECCE centres

items.question: Has the proprietor contacted the relevant District, City or Municipal education office about registration or licensing?
items.action: Engage the relevant Local Government education office and request the official registration/licensing pathway.
items.source_label: ECCE Policy and Standards on Local Government licensing role

items.question: Has the centre prepared documents required for registration or EMIS onboarding?
items.action: Prepare the formal application information, proof of Local Government registration where applicable and the nearest primary school EMIS number.
items.source_label: Guidelines on EMIS onboarding and ECCE centre profiling

items.question: Does the centre have a valid licence or a clear plan to apply for one?
items.action: Prepare for licensing through the relevant Local Government authority and track renewal timelines.
items.source_label: ECCE Standards on licensing and renewal

items.question: Are teacher and caregiver records available and up to date?
items.action: Compile staff lists, qualifications, registration status, contracts and deployment records.
items.source_label: ECCE Policy and Standards on professionalisation and teacher/caregiver registration

items.question: Are pre-primary teachers registered or preparing for registration under the relevant teacher framework?
items.action: Verify teacher registration requirements and support teachers to prepare required records.
items.source_label: ECCE Standards on teacher registration and licensing

items.question: Does the centre have enough adults to safely supervise children?
items.action: Review staffing levels against applicable standards and take corrective action where supervision is weak.
items.source_label: BRMS and ECCE Standards on staffing and supervision

items.question: Does the centre have clear child safety and protection procedures?
items.action: Put in place written child protection procedures, reporting steps and staff orientation.
items.source_label: ECCE Policy provisions on child safety and protection

items.question: Can parents, staff and community members safely report concerns?
items.action: Create a simple complaints and reporting pathway for unsafe environments, unethical behaviour, licensing concerns and child safety issues.
items.source_label: ECCE Standards on complaints and reporting

items.question: Are the premises safe for young children, with obvious hazards identified and managed?
items.action: Check entrances, exits, play areas, classrooms, compounds, sharp objects, unsafe water points and other hazards.
items.source_label: BRMS and ECCE Standards on safety and security

items.question: Does the centre have basic emergency procedures?
items.action: Prepare basic procedures for fire, injury, illness, missing child incidents and emergency contacts.
items.source_label: BRMS on safety, security and emergency preparedness

items.question: Are age-appropriate toilets, handwashing facilities and safe water available?
items.action: Improve sanitation, handwashing and water access before inspection or licensing follow-up.
items.source_label: BRMS and ECCE quality standards on WASH

items.question: Are classrooms, play spaces, toilets and feeding areas clean and child-friendly?
items.action: Set daily cleaning routines and assign responsibility for hygiene monitoring.
items.source_label: BRMS on health, hygiene and safe learning environments

items.question: Is there adequate indoor and outdoor space for play and early learning?
items.action: Review classroom arrangement, outdoor play access and safe movement for children.
items.source_label: BRMS and ECCE Standards on learning environment

items.question: Does the centre have safe, age-appropriate play and learning materials?
items.action: Provide locally available, safe and age-appropriate play materials that support holistic development.
items.source_label: ECCE Policy guidance on play-based learning and holistic development

items.question: Are teachers using age-appropriate, play-based and child-centred methods?
items.action: Orient teachers/caregivers on age-appropriate play, early learning and responsive caregiving practices.
items.source_label: ECCE Policy and curriculum guidance

items.question: Does the centre keep basic child health and emergency contact information?
items.action: Maintain emergency contacts and relevant child health information while protecting confidentiality.
items.source_label: ECCE Policy linkages to health and child wellbeing

items.question: Are feeding arrangements safe, hygienic and appropriate for young children?
items.action: Review feeding arrangements, hygiene, food safety and supervision during meals.
items.source_label: BRMS and ECCE Policy linkages to nutrition

items.question: Does the centre communicate regularly with parents and caregivers?
items.action: Create simple channels for parent communication, meetings, feedback and child progress updates.
items.source_label: ECCE Policy provisions on parent and community engagement

items.question: Does the centre support parents with information on early learning, care, safety and wellbeing?
items.action: Use parent meetings and simple messages to support parenting practices and home learning.
items.source_label: ECCE Policy provisions on parenting programmes

items.question: Are enrolment, attendance, staffing, finance and incident records properly kept?
items.action: Set up simple registers and file systems for core centre records.
items.source_label: BRMS and standards on management and accountability

items.question: Can the centre provide required records and access during routine or unannounced inspection?
items.action: Keep documents accessible and prepare staff to cooperate with inspection and support supervision.
items.source_label: ECCE Standards on routine and unannounced inspections

items.question: Does the centre know where to refer children or families for health, nutrition, protection or sanitation support?
items.action: Identify local referral contacts for health, child protection, nutrition, sanitation and family support.
items.source_label: ECCE Policy provisions on referrals and multisectoral linkages

items.question: Does the centre have a written improvement plan for gaps identified through self-check or inspection?
items.action: Prepare an improvement plan with actions, responsible persons, timelines and support needed.
items.source_label: ECCE implementation guidance on quality improvement and compliance follow-up
